gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: delegateClaim callable by unprivileged EOA — missing role check at proxy level
candidate.claimed_root_cause: Portal.delegateClaim applies only onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH) and no ROLLER_ROLE or DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE check; role enforcement, if any, lives entirely inside the external PORTAL_ROLLER implementation whose source is absent from the repo.
candidate.claimed_impact: Any EOA that is the beneficiary of a launched token can self-trigger LP fee collection at arbitrary frequency, bypassing roller-controlled timing or batching logic and extracting LP fees on demand.

live_preconditions.name: global_switch_bit_set
live_preconditions.observed_value: assumed on (protocol is live and trading)

live_preconditions.name: roller_implementation_lacks_role_check
live_preconditions.observed_value: unknown — PORTAL_ROLLER source not in repo; ROLLER_ROLE constant absent from all indexed source files
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: cast storage 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 <PORTAL_ROLLER_slot> --rpc-url $BSC_RPC; then decompile or fetch verified source for that address from BscScan

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Proxy-level entry point applies only onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH); no ROLLER_ROLE or DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE check present.
NatSpec explicitly states 'Only the roller or default admin can call this function', confirming unrestricted access is not intended behavior.

GUARDIAN_ROLE, TAX_MANAGER_ROLE, TAX_GUARDIAN_ROLE, TOKEN_FLAP_FEE_SETTER_ROLE, MODERATOR_ROLE are all defined here. ROLLER_ROLE is absent — confirmed by grep returning zero matches across entire repo.

claim() also delegates to PORTAL_ROLLER with only onlyIfBitFlagsSet; the role distinction between claim (beneficiary-only) and delegateClaim (roller/admin-only) is architecturally meaningful but not enforced at proxy level for either function.

attacker_path.actor: Unprivileged EOA that is the registered beneficiary of at least one launched token
attacker_inputs: token = address of a token where _tokenBeneficiaries[token] == attacker
call_sequence: 3. _delegateToImpl(PORTAL_ROLLER) executes roller logic in Portal storage context
4. IF roller lacks role check: LP fees are collected and sent to _tokenBeneficiaries[token] (attacker)
5. Attacker repeats at will, bypassing any roller-controlled timing or batching
state_before: Accrued LP fees > 0 in locker/pool position
state_after: LP fees transferred to attacker address
Attacker can repeat immediately without roller authorization

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: LP fees accrued in the V3/V4 locker position for a launched token
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: LP fees sent to beneficiary address controlled by attacker, triggered on-demand without roller authorization
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: No direct protocol loss — fees go to the legitimate beneficiary — but roller-controlled timing/batching invariant is broken; if roller is supposed to gate distribution (e.g., vesting, cooldown, batch scheduling), attacker bypasses that gate
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Entitlement is to receive fees only when a roller or admin triggers the distribution. Attacker self-triggers at arbitrary frequency, extracting fees before the roller-controlled schedule permits, which constitutes premature reward extraction beyond the protocol-intended entitlement window.

setup: Fork BSC at block 108382650 using vm.createSelectFork
Scan LaunchedToDEX and V4LPFeesCollected events to identify a token address with accrued fees
Read _tokenBeneficiaries[token] from Portal storage to confirm beneficiary
Record beneficiary BNB and token balances before call
Impersonate an arbitrary EOA with no roles using vm.prank
expected_assertions: VULNERABLE: call does not revert AND beneficiary balance increases
SAFE: call reverts with NotRoller, AccessControl error, or similar role-check revert
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If call reverts with a role-check error originating inside the PORTAL_ROLLER implementation, the finding is rejected — the roller enforces the access control that the proxy omits.

rejection_reason: not_external_dependency_only gate is set to false because the entire access-control burden for delegateClaim falls on the PORTAL_ROLLER external implementation contract, whose source is not present in this repository and cannot be verified from indexed code alone. The finding is plausible and the proxy-level omission is confirmed, but promotion to HIGH_CONFIDENCE_CANDIDATE requires verifying that the roller implementation also lacks the role check. A fork test against the live BSC deployment is the minimum required proof.
